pokemon:sudowoodo.name: Sudowoodo
pokemon:sudowoodo.summary: Sudowoodo camouflages itself as a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. However, because its hands remain green throughout the year, the Pokémon is easily identified as a fake during the winter.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Sudowoodo camouflages itself as a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. However, because its hands remain green throughout the year, the Pokémon is easily identified as a fake during the winter.
To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.
To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.
If a tree branch shakes when there is no wind, it's a SUDOWOODO, not a tree. It hides from the rain.
Despite appearing to be a tree, its body is closer to rocks and stones. It is very weak to water.
It mimics a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. But since its forelegs remain green throughout the year, it is easily identified as a fake in the winter.
It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.
Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composi­ tion appears to be closer to a rock than a plant.
Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.
Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears to be closer to a rock than a plant.
Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo's extreme aversion to water merits special note.
Apparently, the larger the green parts of this Pokémon, the more collectors value it. It’s a particular favorite among elderly people.
Sudowoodo camouflages itself as a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. However, because its hands remain green throughout the year, the Pokémon is easily identified as a fake during the winter.
It stands along paths pretending to be a tree. If it starts raining, it seems to disappear.
To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.

SUDOWOODO camouflages itself as a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. However, because the forelegs remain green throughout the year, the POKéMON is easily identified as a fake during the winter.

SUDOWOODO camouflages itself as a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. However, because the forelegs remain green throughout the year, the POKéMON is easily identified as a fake during the winter.
It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.
It disguises it­ self as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.
It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.
To avoid attack, it mimics a tree. It will run off if splashed with water, which it hates.
If a tree branch shakes when there is no wind, it’s a Sudowoodo, not a tree. It hides from the rain.
The result of its holding the same pose all the time is arms that have become supple yet strong.
It’s so popular with the elderly that there’s a magazine devoted to this Pokémon. Fans obsess over the particular length and angle of its arms.
To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.
To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.
Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.
It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Sudowoodo camouflages itself as a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. However, because its hands remain green throughout the year, the Pokémon is easily identified as a fake during the winter.

pokemon:bonsly.name: Bonsly
pokemon:bonsly.summary: It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
It looks as if it is always crying. It is actually adjusting its body’s fluid levels by eliminating excess.
In order to adjust the level of fluids in its body, it exudes water from its eyes. This makes it appear to be crying.
Its tears elicit sympathy from those who see them, but do not be deceived! This expulsion of body water is merely a physiological mechanism for keeping itself in good health.
From its eyes, it can expel excess moisture from its body. This liquid is similar in composition to human sweat.
It prefers arid environments. It leaks water from its eyes to adjust its body’s fluid levels.
It prefers arid environments. It leaks water from its eyes to adjust its body’s fluid levels.
It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
This Pokémon lives in dry, rocky areas. As its green spheres dry out, their dull luster increases.
In order to adjust the level of fluids in its body, it exudes water from its eyes. This makes it appear to be crying.
It does not deal well with water, so it lives in dry locales. Since its surroundings tend to lack greenery, it stands out noticeably.
It expels both sweat and tears from its eyes. The sweat is a little salty, while the tears have a slight bitterness.
If its body gets too damp, it will die. So, in a process reminiscent of sweating, its eyes expel moisture.
It discharges moisture from its eyes, making it look like it’s crying—apparently an effective way of getting enemies to let down their guard.
It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
It prefers arid environments. It leaks water from its eyes to adjust its body’s fluid levels.
It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It prefers an arid atmosphere. It leaks water that looks like tears when adjusting its moisture level.
